inputs.prompt: image1 is a sprite sheet strip of four grey mannequins in a row, four frames of one walk cycle animation. Draw the character from image2 four times, once per frame, each copy in the exact pose and camera angle of the mannequin in that frame. Keep the character identical in every frame. The character must face the same direction as the mannequin: facing the viewer, face and both eyes clearly visible. Full body, whole figure visible, feet on the same ground line as the mannequin, soft even lighting, warm highlights, no shadow on the ground, Change background to solid Green color
inputs.instruction: Describe the character and their key features (body shape, physical characteristics, clothing, items, accessories). Then explain how the user's text instruction should alter or modify the character. Generate a new image that meets the user's requirements while maintaining consistency with the original character where appropriate.

inputs.prompt: image1 is a sprite sheet strip of four grey mannequins in a row, four frames of one walk cycle animation. Draw the character from image2 four times, once per frame, each copy in the exact pose and camera angle of the mannequin in that frame. Keep the character identical in every frame. The character must face the same direction as the mannequin: in strict left profile, facing the left edge of the image, nose pointing left. Full body, whole figure visible, feet on the same ground line as the mannequin, soft even lighting, warm highlights, no shadow on the ground, Change background to solid Green color

inputs.prompt: image1 is a sprite sheet strip of four grey mannequins in a row, four frames of one walk cycle animation. Draw the character from image2 four times, once per frame, each copy in the exact pose and camera angle of the mannequin in that frame. Keep the character identical in every frame. The character must face the same direction as the mannequin: facing away from the viewer, showing the back of the head and back, no face visible. Full body, whole figure visible, feet on the same ground line as the mannequin, soft even lighting, warm highlights, no shadow on the ground, Change background to solid Green color

_meta.name: walk_cycle_vnccs
_meta.stage: Warden 4-direction x 4-frame walk cycle (contact-A/passing-A/contact-B/passing-B), batch-per-direction on a single canvas for frame coherence

models.sheet_stage: qwen_image_edit_2511_fp8mixed + qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled TE + qwen_image_vae + QIE2511 Lightning 4-step LoRA (1.0) + VNCCS PoseStudio ART V5.9.5 LoRA (1.0), KSampler 4 steps cfg 1 euler/simple, VAEDecodeTiled
models.bg: solid green via prompt; chroma key done OFFLINE in the deterministic post-chain (no VNCCS chroma node, SAM3 never enabled)

_meta.mannequins: rendered OFFLINE from VNCCS_Utils CharacterData (MakeHuman game_engine rig) with corrected units: matrix.rotx/roty/rotz take DEGREES (server renderer double-converts to radians -> A-pose bug); np.asarray everywhere (np.matrix crash on modelRotation). Eye-face overlay drawn last with a camera-side occlusion rule = unambiguous facing cue. Fixed camera: ground line at 90% cell height, shared scale for all 16 frames; per-frame feet-on-ground + silhouette x-centering before projection. Cell 512x1024, canvas 2048x1024 (4 frames per direction). Yaw: down=0 left=270 right=90 up=180.

gait_keyframes_deg.axis_semantics: thigh/upperarm rz-=forward swing (same sign both sides); calf rz+=knee bend; foot rz+=plantarflex; upperarm_l rx-/upperarm_r rx+=adduct from A-pose; lowerarm rz-=elbow bend; spine_02 rz-=forward lean; mirror = swap _l/_r, negate rx, keep rz
gait_keyframes_deg.note: contact_B/passing_B are sagittal mirrors; down/up rows use flat feet (foot rz=0) — foot pitch cues made QIE draw tiptoe/floating feet in front/back views

_meta.identity_refs: canonical Warden sprites 0005 (down/front), 0004 (up/back), 0006 (left profile), 0006 mirrored (right; no clean right profile exists in the canonical set); bbox-cropped, baked ground shadows scrubbed (dark/red-fleck pixels in bottom 15%), flattened onto green, h=1024
_meta.post_chain: chroma key (green dominance g-max(r,b)>40 & g>90, plus weak-green rule; despill g clamp) -> ground-shadow scrub (rows below 82.5% height: kill dark/desaturated/green-dark/red-fleck px unless bright body px within 6 rows above in-column; hard cut below 92%) -> fixed 4-column frame cut (never per-frame re-centering) -> per-direction registration: shared baseline=median full-res feet row, shared x-center=mean centroid, ONE global scale (tallest direction char height -> 24px) -> area-coverage downsample into 24x32 box (opaque if >=35% cell coverage, per-channel median color of opaque src px), hard alpha, largest-component filter -> shared integer baseline snap (lowest frame feet -> row 29) -> per-direction mediancut quantize <=64 colors (fg only)
_meta.frame_box: 24x32, feet baseline row 29, char height ~22-24px (style-bible sprite_px_height 24); sheet rows down/left/right/up, cols contactA/passingA/contactB/passingB
_meta.run_record: 2026-07-12: facing test 1 frame/direction all correct on first try (eye-cue mannequins + facing clause in prompt). Batch canvases: left/right/up green at seed 20260712; down frame3 rendered as back view -> repair1 (anti-back-view prompt) made frames 3+4 flip -> reseed 20260713 fixed facing; contact feet floated (tiptoe artifact, front view) -> repair2 (feet-planted prompt) gave brown-slipper artifact -> scaffold fix: flat feet on down/up mannequins -> down green @20260713, up repair1 green @20260712. ~33s per canvas on one 3090.
_meta.lessons: front/back rows: keep mannequin feet FLAT (pitch cues become tiptoes); facing flips are seed-luck — reseed beats prompt escalation; do chroma+shadow removal offline, QIE will not reliably omit ground shadows even when told to
